title: VPN Connection Failure
description: User is unable to connect to the corporate VPN. Connection times out or drops immediately after authentication.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the user's internet connection is active and stable.
Step 2: Ask the user to restart the VPN client application.
Step 3: Clear cached VPN credentials by navigating to VPN client settings and selecting 'Clear Saved Credentials'.
Step 4: Ensure the VPN client is updated to the latest version.
Step 5: Check if the user's VPN certificate has expired and renew if necessary.
Step 6: If the issue persists, verify the VPN gateway status on the network monitoring dashboard.
Step 7: Escalate to network infrastructure team if the gateway is unreachable.

title: Network Printer Not Reachable
description: User cannot print to the network printer. The printer appears offline or the print jobs are stuck in the queue.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the printer is powered on and connected to the network.
Step 2: Ping the printer's IP address to confirm network connectivity.
Step 3: Ask the user to remove and re-add the printer in their system settings.
Step 4: Clear the local print spooler by running 'net stop spooler' and 'net start spooler' as administrator.
Step 5: Verify the printer's IP address has not changed by checking the DHCP lease table.
Step 6: Check for any firewall rules blocking port 9100 or 631.
Step 7: If the printer is still unreachable, restart the printer and check the network switch port.

title: DNS Resolution Failure
description: User cannot access internal websites or services. Browser shows DNS resolution errors like 'DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN'.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Run 'nslookup <hostname>' to verify DNS resolution from the user's machine.
Step 2: Flush the DNS cache by running 'ipconfig /flushdns' on Windows or 'sudo dscacheutil -flushcache' on Mac.
Step 3: Verify the user's DNS server settings point to the corporate DNS servers.
Step 4: Check if the DNS issue is widespread or isolated to the user's machine.
Step 5: Restart the DNS client service on the user's workstation.
Step 6: If the issue is widespread, check the corporate DNS server health and logs.
Step 7: Escalate to the network infrastructure team if DNS servers are unresponsive.

title: Application Crashing on Launch
description: A business application crashes immediately upon launch or freezes during startup with an error dialog.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Note the exact error message displayed when the application crashes.
Step 2: Restart the user's workstation and attempt to launch the application again.
Step 3: Check if the application was recently updated — roll back to the previous version if so.
Step 4: Clear the application's cache/temp files from '%APPDATA%\<AppName>'.
Step 5: Repair the application installation via Control Panel > Programs > Repair.
Step 6: Check Windows Event Viewer under Application logs for crash details.
Step 7: Reinstall the application if repair does not resolve the issue.
Step 8: Escalate to the vendor if crashes persist after clean reinstallation.

title: Email Client Not Syncing
description: Outlook or email client is not syncing new emails. User reports missing emails or delayed delivery in their inbox.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the user's internet and network connectivity.
Step 2: Verify the email server status on the monitoring dashboard.
Step 3: Ask the user to restart the Outlook application.
Step 4: Run the Outlook inbox repair tool (scanpst.exe) to fix corrupted OST/PST files.
Step 5: Remove and re-add the email account profile in Outlook.
Step 6: Check if the mailbox is full — archive old emails if over quota.
Step 7: Verify Autodiscover DNS records are resolving correctly.
Step 8: If the issue persists, escalate to the Exchange/email server team.

title: Slow Application Performance
description: User reports that a business application is running extremely slowly, with long load times and unresponsive UI.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the user's system resource usage (CPU, RAM, disk) via Task Manager.
Step 2: Close unnecessary background applications to free up resources.
Step 3: Clear the application's temporary cache and restart it.
Step 4: Verify the network latency to the application server using ping or traceroute.
Step 5: Check the application server's health — CPU, memory, and disk I/O metrics.
Step 6: Review recent application updates or configuration changes that may have degraded performance.
Step 7: If server-side, restart the application service and monitor.
Step 8: Escalate to the development team if the issue is code-related.

title: Password Reset Request
description: User is unable to log in to their account due to a forgotten or expired password and needs a password reset.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the user's identity using the standard identity verification procedure (employee ID, manager confirmation, or security questions).
Step 2: Check if the account is locked out in Active Directory.
Step 3: If locked, unlock the account and reset the password to a temporary value.
Step 4: Set the 'User must change password at next logon' flag.
Step 5: Communicate the temporary password to the user via a secure channel.
Step 6: Confirm the user can log in successfully with the new password.
Step 7: Document the password reset in the incident ticket.

title: Access Permission Request for Shared Drive
description: User requests access to a shared network drive or folder. They receive 'Access Denied' when trying to open the resource.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the user's request has been approved by their manager or resource owner.
Step 2: Identify the correct Active Directory security group for the shared resource.
Step 3: Add the user to the appropriate security group in Active Directory.
Step 4: Ask the user to log off and log back on to refresh their group membership.
Step 5: Verify the user can now access the shared drive.
Step 6: If access is still denied, check NTFS permissions on the folder directly.
Step 7: Document the access grant in the incident ticket and update the access register.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication Issue
description: User is unable to complete MFA verification. The authenticator app is not generating codes or the SMS code is not being received.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the user's identity through an alternate verification method.
Step 2: Check if the user's MFA device (phone) has correct date/time settings — TOTP codes require accurate time.
Step 3: If using an authenticator app, ask the user to remove and re-register the account in the app.
Step 4: Generate a temporary bypass code from the MFA admin portal for immediate access.
Step 5: Re-enroll the user's MFA device in the identity provider portal.
Step 6: Verify the user can successfully authenticate with the new MFA setup.
Step 7: If SMS-based, verify the user's phone number is correct in the directory.

title: Phishing Email Reported
description: User has reported receiving a suspicious phishing email with a malicious link or attachment requesting sensitive information.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Instruct the user NOT to click any links or open any attachments in the email.
Step 2: Ask the user to forward the email as an attachment to the security team's phishing mailbox.
Step 3: Analyze the email headers to identify the sender's true origin.
Step 4: Check the URLs in the email against known threat intelligence feeds.
Step 5: If the user clicked any link, initiate an immediate password reset and scan their workstation.
Step 6: Block the sender's domain and malicious URLs on the email gateway.
Step 7: Send a phishing alert to the organization if the email is part of a wider campaign.
Step 8: Document the incident and update the threat intelligence database.

title: Malware Detected on Workstation
description: Endpoint protection software has detected malware or a virus on the user's workstation. Alerts are showing in the security console.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Immediately isolate the affected workstation from the network.
Step 2: Check the endpoint protection console for details on the malware type and affected files.
Step 3: Run a full system scan using the endpoint protection tool.
Step 4: Quarantine or remove all detected threats.
Step 5: Check if any data exfiltration occurred by reviewing network logs.
Step 6: Reset the user's credentials as a precaution.
Step 7: Reimage the workstation if the malware cannot be fully removed.
Step 8: Reconnect to the network only after a clean scan is confirmed.
Step 9: Document the incident and conduct a root cause analysis.

title: Unauthorized Access Attempt Detected
description: Security monitoring has detected multiple failed login attempts or unauthorized access attempts to a critical system from an unusual location.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Identify the source IP address and geolocation of the unauthorized attempts.
Step 2: Check if the account belongs to a legitimate user and contact them to verify activity.
Step 3: Temporarily lock the targeted account to prevent further attempts.
Step 4: Block the offending IP address on the firewall.
Step 5: Review SIEM logs for related suspicious activity across other systems.
Step 6: Reset the account password and enable additional MFA requirements.
Step 7: Check for any successful logins from the suspicious source and audit actions taken.
Step 8: Document the incident and file a security incident report.

title: Laptop Hardware Failure
description: User reports laptop is not powering on, has a cracked screen, or is experiencing hardware component failures like keyboard or trackpad not working.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Ask the user to describe the exact hardware symptom (no power, screen issue, keyboard, etc.).
Step 2: For power issues — perform a hard reset by holding the power button for 15 seconds, then try again.
Step 3: Try connecting an external monitor to rule out display failure.
Step 4: If under warranty, initiate a warranty repair request with the vendor.
Step 5: If out of warranty, create a hardware replacement request and get cost approval.
Step 6: Provide the user with a loaner device while the laptop is being repaired.
Step 7: Back up user data from the device if possible before sending for repair.
Step 8: Track the repair status and update the user.

title: Monitor Display Issues
description: User's external monitor is not displaying, showing flickering, or has distorted colors. The display is connected but not functioning correctly.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check that the monitor power cable and video cable are securely connected.
Step 2: Try a different video cable (HDMI, DisplayPort, USB-C) to rule out cable failure.
Step 3: Test the monitor with a different computer to isolate the issue.
Step 4: Update the graphics driver on the user's workstation.
Step 5: Adjust the display resolution and refresh rate in Display Settings.
Step 6: If using a docking station, restart the dock and verify firmware is up to date.
Step 7: Replace the monitor if it is confirmed defective.
Step 8: Order a replacement monitor if the current one cannot be repaired.

title: Database Connection Timeout
description: Application is unable to connect to the database. Users see connection timeout errors or 'unable to establish connection' messages.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the database server status — verify it is running and accessible.
Step 2: Test connectivity from the application server to the database using telnet on the DB port.
Step 3: Check if the database connection pool is exhausted — review active connections count.
Step 4: Restart the application's database connection pool.
Step 5: Review database server logs for any errors or resource constraints.
Step 6: Check if there are long-running queries or locks blocking new connections.
Step 7: Kill any stuck sessions if identified.
Step 8: If the database server is overloaded, consider scaling resources or optimizing queries.
Step 9: Escalate to the DBA team for further investigation if the issue persists.

title: Database Replication Lag
description: Database replication between primary and secondary instances is lagging. Read replicas are returning stale data.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the replication lag metric on the database monitoring dashboard.
Step 2: Verify network connectivity and bandwidth between primary and replica servers.
Step 3: Check for long-running write transactions on the primary that may be delaying replication.
Step 4: Review the replica server's resource utilization (CPU, I/O, memory).
Step 5: If the replica is under-resourced, increase its compute capacity.
Step 6: Check for replication errors in the database logs and resolve any conflicts.
Step 7: If lag is critical, consider temporarily redirecting read traffic to the primary.
Step 8: Document the incident and plan for replication architecture review.

title: Cloud VM Instance Not Responding
description: A cloud-hosted virtual machine is not responding to requests. The instance appears running in the console but is unreachable via SSH or RDP.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the VM instance status in the cloud console (GCP/AWS/Azure).
Step 2: Review the instance's serial console output for boot errors or kernel panics.
Step 3: Verify the VM's security group/firewall rules allow inbound SSH (port 22) or RDP (port 3389).
Step 4: Check if the VM's public IP or internal IP has changed.
Step 5: Try stopping and starting the VM instance (not just restarting).
Step 6: If the disk is corrupted, detach and attach it to a recovery instance to retrieve data.
Step 7: Check cloud provider status page for any ongoing incidents in the region.
Step 8: Restore from the latest snapshot if the VM cannot be recovered.

title: Cloud Storage Bucket Access Denied
description: User or application is getting 403 Forbidden errors when trying to access objects in a cloud storage bucket.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the IAM roles and permissions assigned to the user or service account.
Step 2: Check the bucket-level access control policy for any deny rules.
Step 3: Verify the user or service account has the 'Storage Object Viewer' role at minimum.
Step 4: Check if the bucket uses uniform or fine-grained access control and adjust accordingly.
Step 5: If using signed URLs, verify the URL has not expired.
Step 6: Check VPC Service Controls or organization policies that may restrict access.
Step 7: Test access using the cloud CLI tool with the same credentials.
Step 8: Grant the minimum required permissions and verify access is restored.

title: New Employee Onboarding Setup
description: New employee needs IT setup including laptop provisioning, account creation, email setup, and access to required applications and systems.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the new hire details from HR — name, department, start date, role, and manager.
Step 2: Create the Active Directory account with appropriate group memberships based on role.
Step 3: Set up the corporate email account and configure distribution lists.
Step 4: Provision a laptop from inventory and install the standard software image.
Step 5: Configure VPN access and multi-factor authentication.
Step 6: Grant access to required applications, shared drives, and tools based on the role template.
Step 7: Prepare the welcome kit with login credentials and IT quick-start guide.
Step 8: Schedule an IT orientation session for the new employee's first day.
Step 9: Document all provisioned access in the access management system.

title: Software Installation Request
description: User has requested installation of a new software application on their workstation. The software is not available in the self-service portal.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the software request has been approved by the user's manager.
Step 2: Check if the software is on the approved software list.
Step 3: If not approved, submit a software approval request to the IT governance team.
Step 4: Verify license availability — check the license pool or procure a new license if needed.
Step 5: Download the software from the official vendor or internal repository.
Step 6: Install the software remotely using the endpoint management tool or schedule a local installation.
Step 7: Configure the software with the required organizational settings.
Step 8: Verify the software is working correctly and the user can access it.
Step 9: Update the software inventory and license tracking system.

title: Wi-Fi Connectivity Dropping Intermittently
description: User experiences frequent Wi-Fi disconnections throughout the day. The connection drops and reconnects every few minutes.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check if the issue is isolated to one user or affecting multiple users in the same area.
Step 2: Ask the user to forget the Wi-Fi network and reconnect.
Step 3: Update the wireless network adapter driver on the user's device.
Step 4: Check the wireless access point (AP) health in the Wi-Fi controller dashboard.
Step 5: Verify there is no channel interference from neighboring APs or devices.
Step 6: Check the AP's client capacity — it may be overloaded.
Step 7: If the AP is faulty, replace it and reconfigure.
Step 8: Consider adjusting the AP's transmit power or channel assignment.

title: ERP System Login Failure
description: User is unable to log into the ERP system. The login page loads but authentication fails with an 'Invalid credentials' or 'Account disabled' error.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Verify the user is entering the correct username (check for typos or domain prefix).
Step 2: Check if the user's ERP account is active in the ERP admin console.
Step 3: If the account is locked, unlock it and reset the ERP-specific password.
Step 4: Verify the user's role and permissions are correctly assigned in the ERP system.
Step 5: Clear the browser cache and cookies, then retry.
Step 6: Try logging in from a different browser or incognito mode.
Step 7: Check the ERP authentication server logs for detailed error information.
Step 8: Escalate to the ERP vendor support if the issue is system-wide.

title: SSL Certificate Expiry Warning
description: Users are seeing browser security warnings about an expired or invalid SSL certificate when accessing an internal web application.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Identify the affected domain and check the current SSL certificate expiry date.
Step 2: Verify which Certificate Authority (CA) issued the certificate.
Step 3: Generate a new Certificate Signing Request (CSR) for the domain.
Step 4: Submit the CSR to the CA for renewal or issue a new certificate.
Step 5: Install the renewed certificate on the web server or load balancer.
Step 6: Verify the certificate chain is complete (root CA and intermediate certificates).
Step 7: Test the new certificate using an SSL checker tool.
Step 8: Set up automated certificate renewal and expiry alerts to prevent recurrence.

title: Kubernetes Pod CrashLoopBackOff
description: A Kubernetes pod is in CrashLoopBackOff state. The container keeps restarting and the application is unavailable.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Run 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name>' to check events and error messages.
Step 2: Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --previous' to see the last crash output.
Step 3: Verify the container image exists and is accessible from the cluster.
Step 4: Check resource limits — the pod may be OOMKilled due to insufficient memory.
Step 5: Verify environment variables and config maps are correctly mounted.
Step 6: Check liveness and readiness probe configurations — they may be too aggressive.
Step 7: Test the container image locally to reproduce the issue.
Step 8: Fix the root cause (code bug, config error, resource limit) and redeploy.
Step 9: Monitor the pod to ensure it stabilizes after the fix.

title: Database Backup Failure
description: Scheduled database backup job has failed. The backup monitoring alert shows the last successful backup is older than the defined SLA.
resolution_notes:
Step 1: Check the backup job logs to identify the failure reason.
Step 2: Verify there is sufficient disk space on the backup storage target.
Step 3: Check network connectivity between the database server and backup storage.
Step 4: Verify the backup service account credentials have not expired.
Step 5: If the database is too large, check if the backup window is sufficient.
Step 6: Re-run the backup job manually and monitor for errors.
Step 7: If the backup storage is full, archive or delete old backups per the retention policy.
Step 8: Verify the backup completed successfully and test a restore to confirm integrity.
Step 9: Update the backup monitoring alert and document the incident.
